sector_rotation.explanation: No clear rotation signal. Cyclical and defensive sectors are performing similarly — market may be in a transitional phase.

news_headlines.description: It's a breakthrough in Amodei's effort to resolve the company's bitter AI fight with the Pentagon.

news_headlines.title: Stock futures are mixed after Trump says Iran war ‘should be ending pretty soon’: Live updates - CNBC
news_headlines.description: President Donald Trump confirmed on Thursday that the leaders of Israel and Lebanon have agreed to a 10-day ceasefire after officials met in Washington.

news_headlines.title: Chinese carmaker patents voice-controlled 'in-vehicle toilet' - BBC
news_headlines.description: Seres' plans show how stiff competition in the EV space is putting pressure on carmakers to innovate.

news_headlines.title: Trump Says Iran Deal Will Bring ‘Free Oil’ and ‘Free Hormuz Strait’ - WSJ
OpenAI launches new AI model for life sciences research - Axios
news_headlines.description: Researchers are drowning in data. AI could help.

news_headlines.title: Canada Post taking steps to stop home mail delivery - AP News
news_headlines.description: The government-owned corporation that delivers mail in Canada is taking steps to stop home delivery. Canada Post is initiating talks with 13 communities to begin converting about 136,000 addresses from door-to-door delivery to community mailboxes. It is the f…

news_headlines.title: Airline adding bunk beds for economy travelers but bans snacks, smells and cuddling - AP News
news_headlines.description: Air New Zealand will soon add triple-tier bunk beds for economy travelers to have a chance to sleep on long-haul flights. The airline plans to open bookings for the four-hour, lie-flat beds on Boeing 787-9 Dreamliners starting in November. The pods will be on…

news_headlines.title: Stock Market Futures Steady Following Israel-Lebanon Ceasefire News - TipRanks
news_headlines.description: U.S. stock futures steady on Thursday late night despite Donald Trump’s announcement that Israel and Lebanon have reached a 10-day ceasefire agreement. Futures on the Dow Jones Industrial Average (DJIA) and the

news_headlines.title: QVC was once Chester County’s second-largest employer. It has filed for bankruptcy - WHYY
news_headlines.description: The West Chester-based company plans to continue operating and intends to emerge from bankruptcy within 90 days.

news_headlines.title: What lagging jet fuel supplies could mean for airlines and travelers - PBS
news_headlines.description: A looming jet fuel shortage in Europe and Asia sparked by the Iran war and the effective closure of the Strait of Hormuz could further upend world travel within weeks if oil doesn't start flowing again soon — meaning higher airfares and flight cancellations a…

news_headlines.title: Justice Department settles with IBM over alleged DEI practices - The Washington Post
news_headlines.description: After the modest $17 million agreement with the company, officials say they expect it to be the first of many such settlements.

news_headlines.title: SpaceX Moves Up Vesting Schedule for Employee Shares Before IPO - Bloomberg.com
news_headlines.description: SpaceX has moved up a scheduled vesting date for shares awarded to employees to as soon as next week, according to people familiar with the matter, ahead of an IPO targeted to value the company at more than $2 trillion.

news_headlines.title: Airlines cancel flights, ground planes as jet fuel shock hits Europe - politico.eu
news_headlines.description: Lufthansa is retiring 27 planes from its operating fleet, while KLM will cut 80 return flights from Amsterdam Schiphol Airport.

news_headlines.title: Ahead of Tesla’s Q1 Earnings on April 22, Let’s Look at Who Owns TSLA Stock - TipRanks

nyt_headlines.title: Corporate Profits Are at Record Highs. These 4 Factors Could Sink Them.
nyt_headlines.abstract: Experts have different theories about what’s driving steep gains. But many agree the window for growth is shrinking.

nyt_headlines.title: Reopening Strait of Hormuz Would Ease Oil Crisis but Only So Much
nyt_headlines.abstract: Analysts said energy and shipping companies would be reluctant to fully restore operations until they were confident that hostilities were over.

nyt_headlines.title: They Withdrew 401(k) Money Early, and They Have Some Regrets
nyt_headlines.abstract: More Americans are taking hardship withdrawals from workplace retirement accounts, prompted by rising costs — and looser rules.

nyt_headlines.title: The Mighty Engine That Jaguar Built, and Built Jaguar
nyt_headlines.abstract: For 42 years, the XK was Jaguar’s secret weapon. It proved its worth on the racetrack and powered generations of cars. The company’s leap into electrics could take a similar path.

nyt_headlines.title: Federal Court Temporarily Freezes Nexstar’s Merger With Tegna
nyt_headlines.abstract: The judge said the two television companies could not combine operations while an antitrust lawsuit proceeded. Nexstar said its deal was already done.

nyt_headlines.title: American Airlines Says It Won’t Merge With United
nyt_headlines.abstract: American issued the statement after news reports that United Airlines’ chief executive had discussed the idea with the Trump administration.

nyt_headlines.title: Mississippi Liquor Stores Run Dry Amid State Distribution Delays
nyt_headlines.abstract: The state liquor agency has been struggling to make timely deliveries to restaurants, bars and liquor stores since it got rid of outdated conveyor belts at a warehouse in January.

nyt_headlines.title: Sazerac Prepares $15 Billion Cash Offer for Maker of Jack Daniel’s
nyt_headlines.abstract: The Kentucky spirits company’s bid for Brown-Forman comes as the industry is being buffeted by a decline in alcohol consumption.

nyt_headlines.title: Why We’re Concerned About Trump’s Fed Choice
nyt_headlines.abstract: Senators must assess whether Mr. Warsh would perform his duties as a public servant, or whether he would serve the interests of the president.

nyt_headlines.title: Democrats Make Final Push to Delay Confirmation Hearing of Trump’s Fed Pick
nyt_headlines.abstract: Kevin M. Warsh is scheduled to testify on Tuesday despite an ongoing criminal investigation into the chair of the Federal Reserve that stands in the way of a smooth transition.

nyt_headlines.title: White House Shrugs Off Shaky Economy as War Exceeds Trump’s Timeline
nyt_headlines.abstract: Stocks may be soaring again, but the war in Iran has started to pinch the finances of many Americans.

nyt_headlines.title: Jesus Memes, Threats and a War in Iran: A Portrait of Trump Under Pressure
nyt_headlines.abstract: President Trump has lashed out at enemies, allies and even the pope, and made it harder for Republicans to keep the focus on economic issues in a midterm election year.

nyt_headlines.title: Washington’s Scramble to Get Anthropic’s Powerful New Model
nyt_headlines.abstract: Federal agencies have requested access to Claude Mythos Preview, which Anthropic says can rapidly identify — and potentially create — new cyberthreats.
